input.user_input: Summarize the main protections under the Electronic Communications Privacy Act relevant to enterprise SaaS providers.

actual:
The Electronic Communications Privacy Act of 1986 (ECPA) is a primary federal statute governing the privacy of electronic communications. For enterprise Software as a Service (SaaS) providers, the most critical component of ECPA is Title II, known as the Stored Communications Act (SCA). 

Because SaaS providers typically host, store, and process data on behalf of their customers, they generally fall under the SCA’s definitions of an Electronic Communication Service (ECS) or a Remote Computing Service (RCS). 

Here is a summary of the main protections and obligations under ECPA relevant to enterprise SaaS providers:

### 1. Prohibition on Voluntary Disclosure
The SCA establishes a baseline rule that ECS and RCS providers are strictly prohibited from voluntarily disclosing the contents of customer communications to any third party, including the government or private litigants.
